name: unseen_site
split_criterion: The largest non-Haghia-Triada site by inscription count, held out whole = 'Khania' (159 inscriptions). A distinct scribal tradition the candidate never trained on; tests site-transfer, not memorization.
candidate_must_predict: Readings for the multi-sign syllabic word units of every 'Khania' inscription. The correspondence must be fixed BEFORE seeing this site. Scored as token-weighted held-out match rate vs the multi-family null; site-transfer means the reading must not degrade to chance off Haghia Triada.
blocker: No candidate survived the end-to-end multi-family null. WP6 agnostic search and candidate round 1 were both AT_END_TO_END_NULL under the multi-family null (order-shuffle + wrong-language opaque LB + random-prior). West Semitic 'cleared' at 0.030 but the random-prior control also cleared -> artifact, not signal. There is no surviving candidate to score against this seal. The manifest is committed and preserved so any FUTURE candidate is scored on a partition it could not have seen.
non_circular: The split is defined by public metadata (id / site / published formula list / numeral positions / a forthcoming publication), never by any candidate's fit. The held-out payload is committed by commitment_sha256 so it cannot be altered post hoc.
